name: John Clayton
description: American jazz and classical double bassist, arranger and bigband leader
abstract: John Lee Clayton Jr. (born August 20, 1952) is an American jazz musician, classical double bassist, arranger, and composer.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Duffy Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: the bassist for the first three months I was there was John Clayton... we had a very, very great time playing together
about_quotes.doc_title: Duffy Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 17, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: when I was on the band, four and a half years, we had three bass players. They were all named John: it was John Heard, John Duke and John Clayton.
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: I feel very good about a teaching record that has produced everybody from a John Clayton to a Peter Erskine...
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Young Snooky
about_quotes.evidence: I still play with two bands. And they don't think I'm too, they don't hold it against me they want me to come and play with them, you see what I'm saying? Gerald Wilson's band and John Clayton, Clayton-Hamilton Band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Young Snooky — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ramsey Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: I like – the bass player’s son. Kimery: John Clayton? John Clayton’s son, and I like – there’s two other piano players that are around that same age that I like.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ramsey Lewis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: John Clayton is another guy... I thought, well that’s . . . and John Clayton. That band. That absolutely knocked . . . me . . . out.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
